title: Riptide
key: Dm (D natural minor / Dorian-ish color; parallel D major/Lydian at the climax via explicit accidentals, no key-signature change)
feel: driving straight eighths, NOT swung; tempo stated as the eighth-note pulse (150-170 BPM felt per eighth-note grouping per brief) -> Q:1/8=160

ensemble.name: lead
ensemble.register: upper-mid, climbing to high at the climax

theory_hierarchy_declaration.main_theory: D tonal center: D natural minor / Dorian color for the restless build (Intro-B), parallel shift to D major/Lydian for the triumphant climax (C-Outro) -- a mode-mixture 'Picardy' arrival, not a modulation (no key-signature change; climax spelled with explicit accidentals over K:Dm)
theory_hierarchy_declaration.harmony_support: quartal-flavored minor-9/m7 shells in the build (Dm9, Gm7, A7sus4), altered-dominant tension (A7b9, Bb7#11) driving into the climax, open maj7#11/maj9 arrival chords at the climax
theory_hierarchy_declaration.melody_support: one 4-note hook cell (minor-3rd-up-then-stepwise, D-F-G-A) stated as fragment (Intro) -> complete (A1) -> sequenced up a step (A2) -> transformed to a register-leap major statement at the climax (D6->F#6 held)
theory_hierarchy_declaration.texture_support: sparse (keys silent, bass alone) in Intro -> comping stabs enter A1 -> thicker two-stab comping A2-B -> full tutti block chords at climax -> one bar of near-total silence (bar 14) as the subtraction breath right before the climax hits
theory_hierarchy_declaration.groove_support: straight (unswung) driving 7/8 grouped 2+2+3, bass-and-drum riff-lock identity per jazz-funk/fusion style cheatsheet; rhythm density visibly tightens A1->A2->B (more active subdivisions filled) then locks into a tutti hit-pattern at the climax

devices_outside_hierarchy.device: bar-11 full chromatic bass walk-up (7 consecutive semitones) contrary to a fast descending melody run
devices_outside_hierarchy.why: Module 3 'rhythm tightening' + explicit contrary motion as the single strongest pre-climax tension device; used once, not looped

devices_outside_hierarchy.device: bar-13 melody anticipates the D-major tonic a bar early while the harmony (A7b9) is still the dominant
devices_outside_hierarchy.why: chromatic-approach/anticipation device that previews the arrival, per Module 3 'chromatic approach: transisi halus menuju target chord-tone'; used once

arc.journey: restless, unsettled urgency (Intro) -> building tension across A1/A2/B, one breath of subtraction right before the peak -> triumphant, released climax (C) -> a short, confident (not fading) landing tag

phases.requires: low-mid register, sparse texture (keys silent, bass pedal only), off-grid syncopation against the 2+2+3 grouping, hook stated only as a bare fragment, melody stopping on chord tones without a hopeful lift yet

register climbs, rhythm density thickens (more subdivisions filled), bass steps upward then walks a full chromatic run contrary to the melody, altered-dominant tension (b9/#9 color) held and prolonged, one bar (14) of near-total silence as the breath before the hit

full tutti entrance exactly on the downbeat, register leaps to the highest notes of the piece, hook returns transformed into the parallel major with a held peak note, open maj7#11/maj9 chords, on-the-beat confident placement

short 2-bar tag, register eases down slightly from the absolute peak but stays in the bright/major register, ends on a clean sustained final tonic chord -- decisive, matching the brief's 'triumphant... near the end' rather than a long cool-down

hook.description: A 4-note rising cell (minor third up, then stepwise: D-F-G-A) that always arrives via a syncopated pickup landing just ahead of the 7/8 bar's final 3-group (eighth position 4 into position 5) -- the pickup+arrival rhythm is the fixed identity; register, mode (minor->major), and completeness (fragment vs. full vs. leap) are what change across the piece.
hook.rhythm: two rests, a one-eighth pickup on position 4, then the target landing on position 5 (the downbeat of the bar's 3-group) -- either held (fragment) or continued as three quick ascending eighths (complete form)
hook.contour: rising: root, up a minor 3rd, then stepwise up twice (D-F-G-A in the minor statements; D-F#-G#-A i.e. re-spelled as a register-leap in the major climax statement, bar 15)
hook.first_appears: Intro, bar 1, bare (fragment only: pickup into a held target, no ensemble support)

sections.label: Intro
sections.function: establish the groove, the tonal center, and a bare fragment of the hook; keep it lonely

phrasing.note_lengths: one short pickup note into one held target note, then a full bar of silence
phrasing.peak_note: not here -- reserved for the climax
phrasing.placement: syncopated pickup landing just ahead of the bar's 3-group downbeat

sections.tension: melody stops and then the whole ensemble goes silent for a full bar (bar 2) -- tension via emptiness/unresolved space, not harmonic dissonance yet
sections.bass_character: sparse, syncopated pedal (root held across the bar's first 3 units then silent) -- 'lands on beat 1 then leaves space' per the fusion/jazz-funk style cheatsheet

sections.label: A1
sections.function: state the hook in complete form; still restless/minor, still sparse

phrasing.note_lengths: short pickup into three quick ascending eighths (complete hook), then a descending answer that stops on an unresolved tone, then the fragment restated, then a held note that trails into silence
phrasing.contour: rise (complete hook) -> fall (answer, landing on the 2nd/9th) -> rise again (fragment restated) -> held sus tone
phrasing.peak_note: not here -- reserved for the climax
phrasing.placement: same syncopated pickup identity as the Intro
phrasing.motif: hook, complete form (bar 3), then fragment restated (bar 5)

sections.tension: bar 6 ends with a held A7sus4 tone (G, the 4th/7th) that trails into 3 units of silence -- a dominant left hanging, unresolved
sections.bass_character: same sparse syncopated pedal pattern as the Intro, restated -- keeps A1 feeling like a continuation of the same restless idea, not a new idea

sections.label: A2
sections.function: begin the build: register climbs, bass starts moving instead of just pedaling, keys thickens from one stab per bar to two
sections.recurrence: reharmonized + reorchestrated (same hook rhythm, sequenced up a step; bass changes from pedal to stepwise motion; keys changes from one stab to two)

phrasing.note_lengths: pickup + held target (same shape as the Intro fragment, one step higher), then two 2-unit steps climbing to a local peak (Bb5, the highest note so far) and settling
phrasing.contour: rising sequence, reaching a new (temporary) ceiling note, then a held reach with a rest -- tension left open into B
phrasing.peak_note: a temporary ceiling (Bb5) at the end of bar 8/9, NOT the real peak -- deliberately not the highest note of the piece yet
phrasing.placement: on-the-beat, slightly more insistent than A1
phrasing.motif: hook, sequenced up one step from its A1 pitch level

sections.tension: bar 10 melody reaches for Bb5 then C6 (the altered A7b9 tensions) and stops, leaving 2 units of silence before B begins
sections.bass_character: bass steps up: stepwise ascending motion (root-9th-3rd style steps) instead of the earlier pedal, literally 'stepping up' per the building-phase requirement

sections.label: B
sections.function: the hardest push before the climax: rhythm tightens, bass moves in full chromatic contrary motion against a fast descending melody run, the altered dominant is held/prolonged across two bars, then everything drops out for one bar of near-total silence
sections.recurrence: n/a (through-composed peak-approach section, not a repeat)

phrasing.note_lengths: bar 11: six fast single-eighth notes in a descending run (busiest rhythm of the piece so far); bars 12-13: held tension notes and a chromatic inner move toward the destination tonic; bar 14: total silence
phrasing.contour: fast descent (bar 11) -> held altered tone (bar 12) -> chromatic ascent that previews the D-major arrival a bar early (bar 13) -> silence (bar 14)
phrasing.peak_note: not here -- this section is about density and harmonic tension, not register height
phrasing.placement: on-the-beat and dense in bar 11 (rhythm tightening); held/withheld in 12-13; absent in 14
phrasing.motif: no hook restatement here -- deliberately: this section is the one place the hook steps aside so the harmonic/rhythmic tension can be the sole focus (one idea per section, per the compatibility-matrix gate)

sections.tension: the single densest tension stack of the piece: rhythm tightening (bar 11) + bass moving contrary to the melody (bar 11) + a dominant chord held across two full bars (12-13) + a bar of total silence as the 'beat of silence right before a big section' (bar 14) -- four techniques, deliberately all pointed at the same arrival
sections.bass_character: bar 11: a full 7-note chromatic walk-up (B2->F3) moving contrary to the melody's descent -- the single most active bass moment of the piece; bars 12-13: pedal on A (dominant prolongation, deliberately simple so the melody's chromatic motion in bar 13 doesn't collide with a busy bass -- compatibility-matrix check applied); bar 14: one long pedal tone alone, everything else silent

sections.label: C
sections.function: the triumphant, released climax: full ensemble tutti hit exactly on the downbeat, hook returns transformed into the parallel major at the highest register of the piece, held peak note
sections.recurrence: the hook returns transformed: same pickup rhythm as the Intro fragment, but now a register leap (two octaves higher than its first appearance) and re-spelled in the parallel major (F# instead of F) instead of a stepwise climb

phrasing.note_lengths: pickup + long-held peak note (bar 15), then a longer held descending line across the full ensemble sustain (bar 16-18)
phrasing.contour: the biggest rise of the piece (leap to the peak note) then a graceful, still-bright descent
phrasing.peak_note: F#6 (bar 15, held 3 units) is the single highest, most exposed note of the piece, arrived at via a register leap rather than a stepwise climb -- maximizes contrast with every prior section
phrasing.placement: on the beat, confident -- the whole ensemble locks together (kick+snare+keys+bass all hit on the downbeat with the pickup rhythm's landing point)
phrasing.motif: hook, transformed return (register leap + major re-spelling)

sections.tension: release, not build: every device from B (density, chromatic contrary motion, held dominant, silence) resolves here into open maj7#11/maj9 chords, full tutti, and a held peak note -- the arc's payoff
sections.bass_character: full, driving riff-lock with the drums (root-based tutti hits on the downbeat, then arpeggiated triad motion) -- the 'bass-and-drum riff lock' identity the jazz-funk/fusion cheatsheet calls for, now at full density

sections.label: Outro
sections.function: a short, confident landing tag -- NOT a long cool-down, since the brief places the triumphant release 'near the end' rather than asking for a return to calm
sections.recurrence: a compressed recall of the A1/A2 held-dominant-then-rest gesture (bar 6/bar 10), now resolved in the major

phrasing.note_lengths: a short descending 3-note figure, then one long final held tonic for the whole final bar
phrasing.contour: gentle descent from the bright upper register down to the tonic, then stop -- decisive, not trailing off
phrasing.peak_note: n/a -- this section deliberately steps back from the absolute peak by a few scale steps, without abandoning the major/bright register
phrasing.motif: no new hook material -- a cadential tag only

sections.tension: final release: the A7sus4 recall resolves directly into the final Dmaj7#11 tonic, held for the entire last bar with no held-note-into-rest gesture this time (the piece stops instead of trailing off)
sections.bass_character: a final pedal-then-root gesture, ending on a full-bar held tonic under the whole ensemble's final chord

ending: a clean, decisive final Dmaj7#11 tonic chord held for the full final bar across the whole ensemble (no fade, no ritard) -- matches the brief's request for a triumphant, released climax near the end rather than a slow cool-down

Intro.realized: low -- sparse texture, one held-then-silent gesture

A1.realized: low-to-medium -- complete hook stated, but ends on an unresolved held dominant tone into silence
A1.dominant_elements: chord tones, one unresolved sus-tone hold

A2.realized: medium, climbing -- register and bass activity both step up, ends reaching into altered-dominant color

B.realized: high -- densest rhythm, full chromatic contrary-motion bass, two-bar held dominant, then total silence

C.realized: release / peak -- tutti downbeat hit, register-leap peak note, open major/Lydian chords
C.dominant_elements: tonic (parallel major), register climb, open voicing

Outro.planned: settle, but stay confident (not a full return to calm)
Outro.realized: settle -- brief descent then a single held final tonic, no trailing fade
Outro.dominant_elements: tonic, held chord, register down slightly from the peak

interpretive_choices: Brief's tempo phrase '150-170 BPM felt per eighth-note grouping' was read literally as the eighth-note pulse itself (Q:1/8=160 in ABC terms), not the quarter-note pulse -- this is the fastest, most literal reading and is disclosed here because it is a real interpretive fork (an alternative reading would set the quarter note near 80 and feel half as fast).

Meter grouping chosen as 2+2+3 (not 3+2+2 or another split) for the 7/8 bar -- an arbitrary-but-declared choice per groove-meter.md's requirement to 'define the grouping through bass, drums, and melody' rather than leave it ambiguous.

The 'triumphant release' is realized as a parallel-mode shift (D minor/Dorian color -> D major/Lydian, same K:Dm key signature, explicit accidentals) rather than a true modulation to a new key -- a deliberate, cheaper-but-effective device; a true modulation was considered and rejected as unnecessary complexity for a 20-bar piece.

No named groove profile from groove-profiles.md fits this piece: the only profile defined there (neo-soul-core) is an explicitly laid-back pocket, and this piece is straight/driving. Swing is set to 0.5 (straight) in the drum grid instead of inventing an ad hoc numeric profile, per groove-profiles.md's own instruction not to invent one-off tick numbers.
Small ensemble held to exactly the brief's suggestion (lead, keys, bass, drums) with no additional pad/harmony instrument, to keep the interaction map legible at this bar count.
